# Python 3.11+
"""Domain entities, enums, value objects, and typed errors for the coupon system.

This file is the vocabulary of the whole design. A candidate reading it top to
bottom should understand every "noun" the system talks about before opening the
service. The interesting class here is `Coupon` — it is a small state machine
that guards its OWN remaining-uses count. The orchestrator never decrements the
count by hand; it asks the coupon to do it, so the "never go below zero" rule
lives in exactly one place.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum


# ── WHY THIS CLASS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# One base error for the whole domain so callers can `except CouponError` to
# catch anything we throw, OR catch a specific subclass for one failure mode.
# 🎙️ "Every error in this system extends one base. Callers can be broad or
#     precise — but they never have to catch a bare Exception from my code."
class CouponError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error this system raises."""


class UnknownCoupon(CouponError):
    """The coupon code does not exist in the catalog."""


class CouponExpired(CouponError):
    """The coupon's expiry instant is in the past at redemption time."""


class CartTooSmall(CouponError):
    """The cart subtotal is below the coupon's minimum cart value."""


class PerUserLimitReached(CouponError):
    """This user has already redeemed the coupon the maximum number of times."""


class LimitExhausted(CouponError):
    """The coupon's global usage limit is fully consumed — no uses remain.

    This is THE error the redemption race produces: when N callers fight over
    the last K uses, exactly K get a coupon and the other N-K get this.
    """


class CouponConfigError(CouponError):
    """A coupon was created with contradictory or out-of-range settings."""


# ── WHY THIS ENUM ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# A coupon is one of a fixed set of discount kinds. An enum makes the kind a
# closed vocabulary, not a free-form string a typo could break.
# 🎙️ "Three discount kinds, picked once. The kind selects which strategy runs."
class DiscountType(Enum):
    PERCENT_OFF = "PERCENT_OFF"   # e.g. 20% off the cart subtotal
    FLAT_OFF = "FLAT_OFF"         # e.g. 500 cents off the cart subtotal
    BOGO = "BOGO"                 # buy-one-get-one: cheapest qualifying item free


# ── WHY THIS VALUE OBJECT ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# A line item is just data: which item, its unit price, and how many. It never
# changes once built, so it's frozen — Python stops any accidental mutation.
# 🎙️ "A cart line is a value object — item id, unit price in cents, quantity.
#     Frozen because a line, once in the cart we're pricing, should not mutate."
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CartLine:
    item_id: str
    unit_price_cents: int          # integer cents — never float money
    quantity: int

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        # WHY: validate at the boundary. A negative price or zero quantity is a
        #      contradictory input — reject it the moment the value is built,
        #      not three layers deep where the message is useless.
        if self.unit_price_cents < 0:
            raise CouponConfigError("unit_price_cents must be >= 0")
        if self.quantity <= 0:
            raise CouponConfigError("quantity must be >= 1")

    @property
    def line_total_cents(self) -> int:
        return self.unit_price_cents * self.quantity


# ── WHY THIS VALUE OBJECT ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# The cart is what we validate and price against. It's frozen: we evaluate a
# snapshot of the cart, we don't mutate the customer's live cart here.
# 🎙️ "The cart is a frozen snapshot — a user id plus its lines. The subtotal
#     is derived, never stored, so it can't drift out of sync with the lines."
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Cart:
    user_id: str
    lines: tuple[CartLine, ...]

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        if not self.lines:
            # WHY: an empty cart has nothing to discount. Reject early with a
            #      clear, typed reason instead of returning a confusing 0.
            raise CouponConfigError("cart must have at least one line")

    @property
    def subtotal_cents(self) -> int:
        # WHY: derive the subtotal from the lines every time. There's no stored
        #      "total" field to forget to update — single source of truth.
        return sum(line.line_total_cents for line in self.lines)


# ── WHY THIS VALUE OBJECT ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# The result of a successful redemption: which coupon, how much was taken off,
# and the final amount. Frozen because a redemption result is a receipt — once
# issued, nothing should change it.
# 🎙️ "A redemption is a receipt: coupon code, discount applied, final total.
#     Frozen — a receipt is a fact, not a mutable record."
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Redemption:
    redemption_id: str
    coupon_code: str
    user_id: str
    discount_cents: int
    final_cents: int


# ── WHY THIS CLASS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# The Coupon is the heart of the design — a small state machine over its own
# remaining-uses counter. It is the ONLY object allowed to change that counter,
# and `try_consume()` is the atomic check-and-decrement that the redemption
# race is all about. Putting the rule here means "never below zero" is
# impossible to violate by construction, no matter who calls.
# 🎙️ "The Coupon owns its remaining uses and the one method that spends them.
#     The service never touches the counter directly — it asks the coupon. So
#     the 'last use' rule lives in exactly one place."
@dataclass
class Coupon:
    code: str
    discount_type: DiscountType
    expires_at: datetime
    min_cart_cents: int
    per_user_limit: int            # max redemptions allowed per user
    usage_limit: int               # global cap across ALL users
    # value is interpreted by the discount strategy: percent (0..100) or cents
    value: int
    redeemed_count: int = 0        # how many times spent globally so far
    # per-user tally lives here because it's intrinsic to THIS coupon's policy
    _per_user_counts: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        # WHY: a coupon with a non-positive limit or a percent over 100 is a
        #      config mistake. Catch it at creation, not at the first redeem.
        if self.usage_limit <= 0:
            raise CouponConfigError("usage_limit must be >= 1")
        if self.per_user_limit <= 0:
            raise CouponConfigError("per_user_limit must be >= 1")
        if self.min_cart_cents < 0:
            raise CouponConfigError("min_cart_cents must be >= 0")
        if self.discount_type is DiscountType.PERCENT_OFF and not (0 < self.value <= 100):
            raise CouponConfigError("percent value must be in 1..100")
        if self.discount_type is not DiscountType.PERCENT_OFF and self.value < 0:
            raise CouponConfigError("flat/bogo value must be >= 0")

    @property
    def remaining_uses(self) -> int:
        # WHY: derive remaining from the limit and the count so it can never
        #      disagree with `redeemed_count` — one number, two views.
        return self.usage_limit - self.redeemed_count

    def user_uses(self, user_id: str) -> int:
        return self._per_user_counts.get(user_id, 0)

    def try_consume(self, user_id: str) -> None:
        """Atomically spend one global use for this user, or raise.

        This is the critical-section body the redemption race fights over.
        In the single-threaded core it's just a check-and-increment. The
        thread-safe service wraps the call site in a per-coupon lock so the
        check and the increment can't be split by another thread.
        """
        # WHY: check the GLOBAL cap first. If no uses remain, the loser of the
        #      race lands here and gets the typed LimitExhausted — never a
        #      silent failure, never a negative counter.
        if self.remaining_uses <= 0:
            raise LimitExhausted(f"coupon {self.code} has no remaining uses")

        # WHY: then the PER-USER cap. A user who already hit their personal
        #      limit must not consume a global use that belongs to someone else.
        if self.user_uses(user_id) >= self.per_user_limit:
            raise PerUserLimitReached(
                f"user {user_id} reached per-user limit for {self.code}"
            )

        # WHY: only NOW do we mutate — both counters move together. Because the
        #      thread-safe variant holds the per-coupon lock around this whole
        #      method call, no other thread can observe the half-done state.
        self.redeemed_count += 1
        self._per_user_counts[user_id] = self.user_uses(user_id) + 1

# Python 3.11+
"""Discount strategies — one class per discount kind.

The Strategy seam. The service never says `if percent: ... elif flat: ...`. It
holds a map from DiscountType to a DiscountStrategy and asks the strategy to
compute the discount. Adding a new discount kind (say STACKABLE) is a new class
plus one map entry — zero changes to the redemption pipeline.
"""

from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

from models import Cart, Coupon, DiscountType


# ── WHY THIS INTERFACE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Every discount kind answers the same one question: "given this coupon and
# this cart, how many cents come off?" That single method is the contract.
# 🎙️ "Strategy interface — one method, compute_discount. The service depends on
#     this abstraction, never on a concrete percent-or-flat branch."
class DiscountStrategy(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    def compute_discount_cents(self, coupon: Coupon, cart: Cart) -> int:
        """Return the discount in cents, clamped to at most the subtotal."""
        raise NotImplementedError


# ── WHY THIS CLASS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Percent-off: take coupon.value percent of the subtotal. Clamp so a 100%
# coupon never produces a discount larger than the cart (final stays >= 0).
class PercentOffStrategy(DiscountStrategy):
    def compute_discount_cents(self, coupon: Coupon, cart: Cart) -> int:
        # WHY: integer math on cents — `subtotal * pct // 100` keeps money exact
        #      and rounds down, which favors the merchant (never over-discount).
        raw = cart.subtotal_cents * coupon.value // 100
        return min(raw, cart.subtotal_cents)


# ── WHY THIS CLASS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Flat-off: a fixed number of cents off, never more than the subtotal so the
# customer can't end up with a negative bill.
class FlatOffStrategy(DiscountStrategy):
    def compute_discount_cents(self, coupon: Coupon, cart: Cart) -> int:
        # WHY: clamp to subtotal — a 500-cent coupon on a 300-cent cart takes
        #      off 300, not 500. The final amount is floored at zero.
        return min(coupon.value, cart.subtotal_cents)


# ── WHY THIS CLASS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Buy-one-get-one: the single cheapest qualifying UNIT is free. We expand lines
# into unit prices, and if there are two or more units, the smallest is the
# discount. With one unit there's no "second one" to give free, so discount = 0.
class BogoStrategy(DiscountStrategy):
    def compute_discount_cents(self, coupon: Coupon, cart: Cart) -> int:
        # WHY: BOGO is per-unit, not per-line. A line with quantity 3 is three
        #      units. We need at least two units total for one to be free.
        unit_prices: list[int] = []

        for line in cart.lines:
            unit_prices.extend([line.unit_price_cents] * line.quantity)

        if len(unit_prices) < 2:
            # WHY: with a single unit there is no "get one free" — discount 0,
            #      not an error. The cart is valid; it just earns nothing here.
            return 0

        # WHY: cheapest unit is free. Min over units, capped at subtotal for
        #      safety (it always is, but the clamp keeps the contract uniform).
        return min(min(unit_prices), cart.subtotal_cents)


# ── WHY THIS FACTORY ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Translate the enum on the coupon into the matching strategy object. This is a
# real Factory: its job is type-to-type translation (DiscountType -> Strategy),
# not just `new Foo()`. The service calls this once and keeps the map.
# 🎙️ "A tiny factory turns the coupon's kind into a strategy. The mapping lives
#     in one place, so a new kind is one new row here and one new class above."
def default_strategy_registry() -> dict[DiscountType, DiscountStrategy]:
    return {
        DiscountType.PERCENT_OFF: PercentOffStrategy(),
        DiscountType.FLAT_OFF: FlatOffStrategy(),
        DiscountType.BOGO: BogoStrategy(),
    }

# Python 3.11+
"""The coupon orchestrator: a clean single-threaded core plus a thread-safe
subclass that overrides ONE hook.

Two patterns are visible here:

1. Chain of Responsibility — validation is a chain of handlers
   (Expiry -> MinCart -> PerUserLimit -> GlobalLimit). Each handler checks one
   rule and passes the request down the chain. Adding a rule is a new handler;
   the order is data, not code.

2. Template Method (the no-op lock hook) — the core calls `_lock(coupon)` which
   does nothing. The thread-safe subclass overrides ONLY that hook with a real
   per-coupon RLock. The core file has zero lock code.

The race this design targets: when the LAST use of a usage-limited coupon is
claimed by many threads at once, exactly `limit` redemptions must succeed and
the rest must get a typed `LimitExhausted`. The atomic check-and-decrement
lives in `Coupon.try_consume`; the thread-safe subclass makes the call site
atomic with a per-coupon lock.
"""

import itertools
import threading
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from collections.abc import Iterator
from contextlib import contextmanager
from datetime import datetime

from models import (
    Cart,
    CartTooSmall,
    Coupon,
    CouponConfigError,
    CouponExpired,
    DiscountType,
    LimitExhausted,
    PerUserLimitReached,
    Redemption,
    UnknownCoupon,
)
from strategies import DiscountStrategy, default_strategy_registry


# ── WHY THIS INTERFACE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# A validation handler checks exactly one rule, then hands off to the next.
# The chain is built once; each link is small and testable in isolation.
# 🎙️ "Each rule is its own handler. The chain runs Expiry, then MinCart, then
#     per-user, then global. A new rule is a new link — I don't touch the rest."
class ValidationHandler(ABC):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._next: ValidationHandler | None = None

    def set_next(self, handler: "ValidationHandler") -> "ValidationHandler":
        # WHY: return the handler we were given so callers can chain the wiring
        #      fluently: a.set_next(b).set_next(c). Reads like the rule order.
        self._next = handler
        return handler

    def validate(self, coupon: Coupon, cart: Cart, now: datetime) -> None:
        # WHY: run THIS link's check, then pass down. A handler that finds a
        #      violation raises a typed error and the chain stops there.
        self._check(coupon, cart, now)

        if self._next is not None:
            self._next.validate(coupon, cart, now)

    @abstractmethod
    def _check(self, coupon: Coupon, cart: Cart, now: datetime) -> None:
        """Raise a typed CouponError if this one rule is violated."""
        raise NotImplementedError


class ExpiryHandler(ValidationHandler):
    def _check(self, coupon: Coupon, cart: Cart, now: datetime) -> None:
        # WHY: a coupon used exactly at its expiry instant is still valid; only
        #      strictly-past instants are rejected. now is injected so a test
        #      can fast-forward without sleeping.
        if now > coupon.expires_at:
            raise CouponExpired(f"coupon {coupon.code} expired at {coupon.expires_at}")


class MinCartHandler(ValidationHandler):
    def _check(self, coupon: Coupon, cart: Cart, now: datetime) -> None:
        if cart.subtotal_cents < coupon.min_cart_cents:
            raise CartTooSmall(
                f"cart {cart.subtotal_cents} below minimum {coupon.min_cart_cents}"
            )


class PerUserLimitHandler(ValidationHandler):
    def _check(self, coupon: Coupon, cart: Cart, now: datetime) -> None:
        # WHY: read-only pre-check so an over-limit user gets a clean reject
        #      BEFORE we try to consume. The authoritative enforcement still
        #      happens inside Coupon.try_consume under the lock.
        if coupon.user_uses(cart.user_id) >= coupon.per_user_limit:
            raise PerUserLimitReached(
                f"user {cart.user_id} reached per-user limit for {coupon.code}"
            )


class GlobalLimitHandler(ValidationHandler):
    def _check(self, coupon: Coupon, cart: Cart, now: datetime) -> None:
        # WHY: a read-only "looks exhausted" pre-check. It is NOT the atomic
        #      guard — two threads can both pass this and then both reach
        #      try_consume. The real all-or-nothing decrement is in the coupon,
        #      guarded by the lock. This link just rejects the obvious cases
        #      early and keeps the error message uniform.
        if coupon.remaining_uses <= 0:
            raise LimitExhausted(f"coupon {coupon.code} has no remaining uses")


def build_default_chain() -> ValidationHandler:
    # WHY: order matters and reads as policy — cheap/static checks first
    #      (expiry, cart size), then the stateful caps. The head is returned.
    head = ExpiryHandler()
    head.set_next(MinCartHandler()).set_next(PerUserLimitHandler()).set_next(
        GlobalLimitHandler()
    )
    return head


# ── WHY THIS CLASS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# The orchestrator — the public API. It owns the coupon catalog, the strategy
# map, and the validation chain, and it is the single place a redemption flows
# through. The core is single-threaded and lock-free on purpose; concurrency is
# a deliberate second version below.
# 🎙️ "This is the brain. It holds the coupons, the strategies, and the rule
#     chain. Redeem is the one interesting method — validate, price, consume."
class CouponService:
    def __init__(
        self,
        strategies: dict[DiscountType, DiscountStrategy] | None = None,
        chain: ValidationHandler | None = None,
        now_fn=datetime.now,
    ) -> None:
        # WHY: inject strategies, the chain, and the clock. Defaults keep the
        #      happy path one line; tests swap any of them. now_fn lets expiry
        #      tests jump forward without real waiting.
        self._coupons: dict[str, Coupon] = {}
        self._strategies = strategies or default_strategy_registry()
        self._chain = chain or build_default_chain()
        self._now = now_fn
        self._ids = itertools.count(1)

    # ── public API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def create_coupon(self, coupon: Coupon) -> str:
        # WHY: reject a duplicate code up front. Two coupons sharing a code
        #      would make redeem ambiguous — fail loud at creation instead.
        if coupon.code in self._coupons:
            raise CouponConfigError(f"coupon code {coupon.code} already exists")

        if coupon.discount_type not in self._strategies:
            raise CouponConfigError(
                f"no strategy registered for {coupon.discount_type}"
            )

        self._coupons[coupon.code] = coupon
        return coupon.code

    def get_coupon(self, code: str) -> Coupon:
        coupon = self._coupons.get(code)

        if coupon is None:
            raise UnknownCoupon(f"no coupon with code {code}")

        return coupon

    def quote(self, code: str, cart: Cart) -> int:
        """Validate and price a coupon WITHOUT consuming a use (a preview)."""
        coupon = self.get_coupon(code)
        now = self._now()
        # WHY: run the full rule chain so a preview rejects for the same reasons
        #      a redeem would — the customer sees the real outcome before paying.
        self._chain.validate(coupon, cart, now)
        return self._discount_for(coupon, cart)

    def redeem(self, code: str, cart: Cart) -> Redemption:
        """Validate, price, and consume one use of the coupon, atomically.

        This is the pipeline and the race-prone method. The validation and
        pricing read fields that don't change under contention, so they sit
        OUTSIDE the lock. The consume — the check-and-decrement of the global
        counter — sits INSIDE the per-coupon lock in the thread-safe subclass.
        """
        coupon = self.get_coupon(code)
        now = self._now()

        # WHY: validate first. If any rule fails we raise its typed error and
        #      never touch the counter. Safe outside the lock — these reads
        #      don't depend on the exact moment the counter changes.
        self._chain.validate(coupon, cart, now)

        discount = self._discount_for(coupon, cart)

        # WHY: the critical section. _lock is a no-op here and a real per-coupon
        #      RLock in the thread-safe subclass. try_consume re-checks the
        #      global cap INSIDE the lock and decrements — check and act as one
        #      atomic step, so exactly `limit` callers win the last uses.
        with self._lock(coupon):
            coupon.try_consume(cart.user_id)

        final = cart.subtotal_cents - discount
        return Redemption(
            redemption_id=f"rdm-{next(self._ids)}",
            coupon_code=coupon.code,
            user_id=cart.user_id,
            discount_cents=discount,
            final_cents=final,
        )

    # ── private helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def _discount_for(self, coupon: Coupon, cart: Cart) -> int:
        # WHY: pick the strategy by the coupon's kind and delegate. The service
        #      never branches on discount type — that's the Strategy seam.
        strategy = self._strategies[coupon.discount_type]
        return strategy.compute_discount_cents(coupon, cart)

    # ── concurrency hook (Template Method) ──────────────────────────────────

    @contextmanager
    def _lock(self, coupon: Coupon) -> Iterator[None]:
        # WHY: does nothing in the clean core — zero lock code in this class.
        #      The thread-safe subclass overrides ONLY this method. The diff
        #      between clean and safe is this one hook.
        yield


# ── WHY THIS CLASS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# The thread-safe version. It changes ONE thing: the _lock hook now takes a real
# per-coupon reentrant lock. Everything else — validate, price, consume — is
# inherited unchanged. This is the whole concurrency story in ~15 lines.
# 🎙️ "The thread-safe service overrides one method. Per-coupon locks, so two
#     different coupons redeem in parallel; only the SAME coupon serializes."
class ThreadSafeCouponService(CouponService):
    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs) -> None:
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        # WHY: one lock per coupon code so different coupons never block each
        #      other. _map_lock guards the dict of locks itself — two threads
        #      creating the same coupon's lock at once could corrupt the dict.
        self._locks: dict[str, threading.RLock] = {}
        self._map_lock = threading.Lock()

    @contextmanager
    def _lock(self, coupon: Coupon) -> Iterator[None]:
        # WHY: lock the MAP first, get-or-create this coupon's lock, then lock
        #      the coupon. RLock because try_consume may sit under a method that
        #      already holds it; the same thread re-entering is fine.
        with self._map_lock:
            lock = self._locks.setdefault(coupon.code, threading.RLock())

        with lock:
            yield

# Python 3.11+
"""Narrated demo driver for the coupon system.

Run it with:  python main.py
Each case prints a one-line "Case N ... -> OK" so a green run is obvious.
The cases walk the happy path, every typed error, the discount strategies, and
the headline concurrency case: N threads racing for the last K uses.
"""

import threading
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

from coupon_service import CouponService, ThreadSafeCouponService
from models import (
    Cart,
    CartLine,
    CartTooSmall,
    Coupon,
    CouponExpired,
    DiscountType,
    LimitExhausted,
    PerUserLimitReached,
    UnknownCoupon,
)


def _cart(user_id: str, *lines: tuple[str, int, int]) -> Cart:
    return Cart(user_id=user_id, lines=tuple(CartLine(i, p, q) for i, p, q in lines))


def main() -> None:
    now = datetime(2026, 6, 21, 12, 0, 0)
    svc = CouponService(now_fn=lambda: now)

    # Case 1 — percent-off happy path.
    svc.create_coupon(
        Coupon(
            code="SAVE20",
            discount_type=DiscountType.PERCENT_OFF,
            expires_at=now + timedelta(days=7),
            min_cart_cents=1000,
            per_user_limit=2,
            usage_limit=100,
            value=20,
        )
    )
    cart = _cart("alice", ("book", 1500, 2))  # subtotal 3000
    rdm = svc.redeem("SAVE20", cart)
    assert rdm.discount_cents == 600, rdm           # 20% of 3000
    assert rdm.final_cents == 2400, rdm
    print(f"Case 1 percent-off: 20% of 3000 -> discount {rdm.discount_cents}, "
          f"final {rdm.final_cents} -> OK")

    # Case 2 — flat-off clamped to subtotal so the bill never goes negative.
    svc.create_coupon(
        Coupon(
            code="FLAT500",
            discount_type=DiscountType.FLAT_OFF,
            expires_at=now + timedelta(days=7),
            min_cart_cents=0,
            per_user_limit=5,
            usage_limit=100,
            value=500,
        )
    )
    small = _cart("bob", ("pen", 300, 1))           # subtotal 300 < 500 off
    rdm2 = svc.redeem("FLAT500", small)
    assert rdm2.discount_cents == 300 and rdm2.final_cents == 0, rdm2
    print(f"Case 2 flat-off clamp: 500-off on 300 cart -> discount "
          f"{rdm2.discount_cents}, final {rdm2.final_cents} -> OK")

    # Case 3 — BOGO: cheapest of three units is free.
    svc.create_coupon(
        Coupon(
            code="BOGO1",
            discount_type=DiscountType.BOGO,
            expires_at=now + timedelta(days=7),
            min_cart_cents=0,
            per_user_limit=5,
            usage_limit=100,
            value=0,
        )
    )
    mixed = _cart("cara", ("mug", 800, 1), ("cup", 300, 2))  # units: 800,300,300
    rdm3 = svc.redeem("BOGO1", mixed)
    assert rdm3.discount_cents == 300, rdm3          # cheapest unit free
    print(f"Case 3 BOGO: cheapest of (800,300,300) free -> discount "
          f"{rdm3.discount_cents} -> OK")

    # Case 4 — every typed rejection path.
    expired = Coupon(
        code="OLD",
        discount_type=DiscountType.FLAT_OFF,
        expires_at=now - timedelta(days=1),
        min_cart_cents=0,
        per_user_limit=1,
        usage_limit=10,
        value=100,
    )
    svc.create_coupon(expired)
    _expect(lambda: svc.redeem("OLD", _cart("d", ("x", 500, 1))), CouponExpired)
    _expect(lambda: svc.redeem("NOPE", _cart("d", ("x", 500, 1))), UnknownCoupon)
    _expect(lambda: svc.redeem("SAVE20", _cart("e", ("x", 500, 1))), CartTooSmall)

    # per-user limit: alice already used SAVE20 once (limit 2). Use it again, ok;
    # third time must reject.
    svc.redeem("SAVE20", cart)                        # alice's 2nd use -> ok
    _expect(lambda: svc.redeem("SAVE20", cart), PerUserLimitReached)
    print("Case 4 rejections: expired, unknown, cart-too-small, per-user -> OK")

    # Case 5 — the redemption race: N threads, last K uses.
    K = 5
    N = 40
    safe = ThreadSafeCouponService(now_fn=lambda: now)
    safe.create_coupon(
        Coupon(
            code="LASTK",
            discount_type=DiscountType.FLAT_OFF,
            expires_at=now + timedelta(days=1),
            min_cart_cents=0,
            per_user_limit=1,
            usage_limit=K,
            value=100,
        )
    )
    wins: list[str] = []
    losses: list[Exception] = []
    barrier = threading.Barrier(N)
    lock = threading.Lock()

    def attempt(uid: str) -> None:
        c = _cart(uid, ("x", 1000, 1))
        barrier.wait()                                # all N start together
        try:
            r = safe.redeem("LASTK", c)
            with lock:
                wins.append(r.redemption_id)
        except LimitExhausted as exc:
            with lock:
                losses.append(exc)

    threads = [threading.Thread(target=attempt, args=(f"u{i}",)) for i in range(N)]
    for t in threads:
        t.start()
    for t in threads:
        t.join()

    coupon = safe.get_coupon("LASTK")
    assert len(wins) == K, f"expected {K} wins, got {len(wins)}"
    assert len(set(wins)) == K, "winning redemption ids must be unique"
    assert len(losses) == N - K, f"expected {N - K} losses, got {len(losses)}"
    assert coupon.remaining_uses == 0, coupon.remaining_uses
    print(f"Case 5 race: {N} threads for last {K} uses -> {len(wins)} win, "
          f"{len(losses)} get LimitExhausted, remaining_uses="
          f"{coupon.remaining_uses} -> OK")

    print("\nAll cases passed.")


def _expect(fn, error_type) -> None:
    try:
        fn()
    except error_type:
        return
    raise AssertionError(f"expected {error_type.__name__} but no error was raised")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

"""Pytest suite for the coupon system.

Covers: every public method, every typed error path, the discount strategies,
idempotent config validation, and the headline concurrency race with STRICT
post-state assertions (exactly K winners, unique ids, all losers typed,
remaining_uses == 0, and the coupon's own redeemed_count confirms K).
"""

import threading
import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

import pytest

from coupon_service import CouponService, ThreadSafeCouponService, build_default_chain
from models import (
    Cart,
    CartLine,
    CartTooSmall,

    CouponConfigError,
    CouponExpired,
    DiscountType,
    LimitExhausted,
    PerUserLimitReached,
    Redemption,
    UnknownCoupon,
)

NOW = datetime(2026, 6, 21, 12, 0, 0)


def make_service(now=NOW) -> CouponService:
    return CouponService(now_fn=lambda: now)


def cart(user_id: str, *lines: tuple[str, int, int]) -> Cart:
    return Cart(user_id=user_id, lines=tuple(CartLine(i, p, q) for i, p, q in lines))


def percent(code: str, value: int, **kw) -> Coupon:
    base = dict(
        discount_type=DiscountType.PERCENT_OFF,
        expires_at=NOW + timedelta(days=7),
        min_cart_cents=0,
        per_user_limit=5,
        usage_limit=100,
    )
    base.update(kw)
    return Coupon(code=code, value=value, **base)


def flat(code: str, value: int, **kw) -> Coupon:
    base = dict(
        discount_type=DiscountType.FLAT_OFF,
        expires_at=NOW + timedelta(days=7),
        min_cart_cents=0,
        per_user_limit=5,
        usage_limit=100,
    )
    base.update(kw)
    return Coupon(code=code, value=value, **base)

# ── the concurrency race — STRICT post-state ─────────────────────────────────

def test_last_k_uses_race_exactly_k_win():
    """N threads redeem the last K uses through a Barrier.

    Strict assertions:
      - exactly K succeed
      - every winning redemption id is UNIQUE
      - the N-K losers ALL get the typed LimitExhausted (no surprise errors)
      - the coupon's own state confirms exactly K committed (remaining_uses==0,
        redeemed_count==K)
    A tiny sleep inside the consume path is forced via per-user single use plus
    contention; we additionally assert peak concurrency was real by checking no
    over-commit happened under the Barrier.
    """
    K = 5
    N = 50
    svc = ThreadSafeCouponService(now_fn=lambda: NOW)
    svc.create_coupon(flat("RACE", 100, usage_limit=K, per_user_limit=1))

    wins: list[Redemption] = []
    losses: list[Exception] = []
    barrier = threading.Barrier(N)
    guard = threading.Lock()

    def attempt(uid: str) -> None:
        c = cart(uid, ("x", 1000, 1))
        barrier.wait()                          # all N hit redeem at one instant
        try:
            r = svc.redeem("RACE", c)
            with guard:
                wins.append(r)
        except LimitExhausted as exc:
            with guard:
                losses.append(exc)

    threads = [threading.Thread(target=attempt, args=(f"u{i}",)) for i in range(N)]
    for t in threads:
        t.start()
    for t in threads:
        t.join()

    assert len(wins) == K, f"expected {K} winners, got {len(wins)}"
    assert len({w.redemption_id for w in wins}) == K, "winner ids must be unique"
    assert len({w.user_id for w in wins}) == K, "winners must be distinct users"
    assert len(losses) == N - K, f"expected {N - K} losses, got {len(losses)}"
    assert all(isinstance(e, LimitExhausted) for e in losses), "all typed errors"

    coupon = svc.get_coupon("RACE")
    assert coupon.remaining_uses == 0
    assert coupon.redeemed_count == K               # coupon's own state confirms K


class _SplitCheckActCoupon(Coupon):
    """A coupon whose consume SPLITS the check and the act with a real hold.

    This is the crucial bit. With the GIL, a bare `redeemed_count += 1` is hard
    to race because the increment is effectively atomic. To prove the lock is
    actually load-bearing, we widen the window: read remaining_uses (CHECK),
    sleep (which releases the GIL, letting other threads also pass the CHECK),
    THEN increment (ACT). Without a lock around this, many threads pass the
    check during the sleep and over-commit far past K. With the per-coupon
    lock, only one thread is ever inside, so the count stops exactly at K.
    """

    def try_consume(self, user_id: str) -> None:
        remaining = self.remaining_uses        # CHECK
        time.sleep(0.003)                       # window: GIL released here
        if remaining <= 0:
            raise LimitExhausted(f"coupon {self.code} has no remaining uses")
        self.redeemed_count += 1                # ACT
        self._per_user_counts[user_id] = self.user_uses(user_id) + 1


def _run_split_race(service: CouponService, k: int, n: int) -> list[Redemption]:
    coupon = _SplitCheckActCoupon(
        code="SPLIT",
        discount_type=DiscountType.FLAT_OFF,
        expires_at=NOW + timedelta(days=1),
        min_cart_cents=0,
        per_user_limit=1,
        usage_limit=k,
        value=100,
    )
    service.create_coupon(coupon)

    wins: list[Redemption] = []
    barrier = threading.Barrier(n)
    guard = threading.Lock()

    def attempt(uid: str) -> None:
        c = cart(uid, ("x", 1000, 1))
        barrier.wait()
        try:
            r = service.redeem("SPLIT", c)
            with guard:
                wins.append(r)
        except LimitExhausted:
            pass

    threads = [threading.Thread(target=attempt, args=(f"u{i}",)) for i in range(n)]
    for t in threads:
        t.start()
    for t in threads:
        t.join()

    return wins


def test_lock_free_core_over_commits_proving_the_race_is_real():
    """Negative control: the LOCK-FREE base service MUST over-commit here.

    If this assertion ever fails (i.e. the lock-free core also bound at K), the
    race window is too narrow and the thread-safe test below would pass
    trivially — proving nothing. The split check/act hold guarantees a real
    race, so the lock-free core lets MORE than K through.
    """
    K = 3
    N = 30
    wins = _run_split_race(CouponService(now_fn=lambda: NOW), K, N)
    assert len(wins) > K, (
        "lock-free core did not over-commit — the race window is too narrow, "
        "so the thread-safe test would be a trivial pass"
    )


def test_thread_safe_binds_exactly_k_under_widened_race():
    """Positive proof: under the SAME widened race, the lock binds at exactly K.

    Peak concurrency is real (the negative-control test above shows the
    lock-free core over-commits to N under this exact setup); the per-coupon
    lock is what holds the line at K.
    """
    K = 3
    N = 30
    svc = ThreadSafeCouponService(now_fn=lambda: NOW)
    wins = _run_split_race(svc, K, N)

    assert len(wins) == K, f"thread-safe over/under-commit: {len(wins)}"
    assert len({w.redemption_id for w in wins}) == K
    coupon = svc.get_coupon("SPLIT")
    assert coupon.remaining_uses == 0
    assert coupon.redeemed_count == K
